business.headline: DA hike: Why did bank employees get only 0.70% while govt staff received 2%?A small DA hike for bank staff and a bigger one for government employees, has raised eyebrows. But is the difference unfair, or is there more to the calculation behind the scenes? Let us know here.

Explained: Why Paytm share price surged over 6% todayPaytm shares rose over 6% after One 97 Communications reported a Q4 profit and its first annual profit. The results signalled improving payments growth and margins even as regulatory concerns remained in focus.

Sensex, Nifty edge higher as softer crude lifts Dalal Street moodThe BSE Sensex rose 27.75 points, or 0.04%, to 77,986.27 in early trade, while the Nifty 50 gained 19.30 points, or 0.08%, to trade at 24,350.25 around 9:30 am.

8th Pay Commission: Govt employees push for 5-year pay hikes. Is it practical?As part of its exclusive 8th Pay Commission coverage, IndiaToday.in spoke to union leaders demanding salary revisions every five years. We also asked experts whether such a move would help employees or strain government finances further.

8th Pay Commission update: Are defence and railway unions ready for May 10 deadline?In its latest circular dated May 6, 2026, the Commission said it will meet unions and associations linked to the Defence and Railway sectors in Delhi on May 13 and 14, 2026.

Can the US CLARITY Act finally bring clear rules to the crypto industry?For years, crypto has grown fast but without clear rules. The US CLARITY Act aims to fix that gap. Will it finally bring order to the digital asset space?

Internal SAIL notice shows plan to cut contract workforce by 40%. Read detailsAn internal SAIL (Steel Authority of India Limited) communication reviewed by India Today sheds light on why the PSU is planning a 40% reduction in contract workforce by 2027 and why unions are opposing the move.

HDFC Bank governance review finds no major lapses after chairman exit: ReportAn internal review at HDFC Bank found no material governance lapses after Atanu Chakraborty's exit. The findings are expected to steady investor confidence and clear the way for the CEO term proposal.
Sensex closes 940 points higher, Nifty jumps 24,300; IndiGo up 7%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 940.73 points to close at 77,958.52, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 298.15 points to end at 24,330.95.

Salary hike or bonus in hand: Should you invest for returns or repay your home loan?A salary hike or bonus often feels like a reward, but it also brings a tough choice. Should you invest it to grow your wealth or put it towards repaying your home loan faster? Let's find out here.

Mukesh Ambani-led Reliance plans India's answer to Starlink: ReportReliance Industries is exploring a multi-billion-dollar LEO satellite venture under Jio Platforms to build an India-based rival to Starlink. The move could sharpen the satellite internet race as India pushes for domestic space and broadband capacity.

Govt approves ECLGS 5.0 to support MSMEs, airlines amid global tensionsUnder Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme 5.0, the government will offer 100% credit guarantee for MSMEs and 90% for non-MSMEs and airlines.

Confused about ITR filing, refunds or deductions? 'Kar Saathi' has answersFiling your ITR and stuck with questions on refunds or deductions? You're not alone—and now there's an easier way to get help. Could 'Kar Saathi' be the answer you've been looking for?

Memes, reels and trades: How social media hype is driving risky stock betsBillionaire investor Warren Buffett recently said in a podcast that he no longer fully understands parts of today's market and even likened it to a casino for investors. IndiaToday.in spoke to experts to understand whether markets are increasingly being driven by speculation rather than fundamentals.

Why do government employees want Pay Commission every 5 years instead of 10?As part of its continuing exclusive coverage of the 8th Pay Commission discussions, IndiaToday.in has learnt that one of the major suggestions raised during these meetings was to reduce the gap between two Pay Commissions from 10 years to 5 years.

Sensex, Nifty open higher as oil drops on Trump's Iran peace signalBenchmark indices opened higher on Wednesday as lower oil prices and earnings-led moves boosted sentiment. The rise came with caution, as analysts flagged credibility concerns around Iran-related signals and stretched broader-market valuations.

G7 in talks to set up permanent body to oversee critical minerals supplyG7 countries are discussing a permanent secretariat to carry forward critical minerals initiatives beyond rotating presidencies. The talks lay bare differences over stockpiling and leadership as members try to reduce reliance on China.

Ethanol gets price protection, maize doesn't: What's going wrong?The government has set the MSP for maize at Rs 2,400 per quintal. However, in mandis, farmers are often forced to sell at much lower rates.

Cognizant eyes up to 15,000 job cuts, India likely to see biggest impactThe possible job cuts come at a time when many large IT firms are restructuring. Slower spending by clients, rising automation, and the growing use of AI are changing the way companies operate.

Zee files case against Nykaa for alleged misuse of songs, seeks $210,000 damagesZee Entertainment has filed a lawsuit in the Delhi High Court, seeking damages of $210,000. The company claims that Nykaa used several of its copyrighted songs in Instagram reels to promote products to its large online audience.

entertainment.headline: HBO renews Harry Potter series for second season ahead of first episode release HBO has renewed its upcoming Harry Potter series for a second season months before the release of the first instalment. The next chapter is expected to adapt Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets, with filming set to begin this autumn.

Allu Arjun's wife, father visit injured Pushpa 2 stampede victim, promise support Allu Aravind and Allu Arjun's wife Sneha Reddy visited injured Sritej's home and met his family. The family promised to extend medical and educational support as he continues rehabilitation.

After BJP's Bengal win, Vivek Agnihotri's The Bengal Files to release in the state Vivek Agnihotri's The Bengal Files is set to be screened in Kolkata from Friday after months of release trouble in West Bengal. The move follows his allegations that the film had been blocked in the state and plans to add more theatres across Bengal.

Vetri prophecy? How Vijay and his father's first film predicted TVK's historic win From a child actor in Vetri to leading Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam to victory, Vijay's journey feels almost cinematic. The relationship between his 1984 debut film and his 2026 political triumph has now become part of Tamil Nadu's political folklore.

Elle teaser: Legally Blonde prequel has Elle Woods navigating teenage Lexi Minetree stars as Elle Woods in Elle, a prequel to Reese Witherspoon's film, Legally Blonde. Prime Video has unveiled first-look images and a teaser trailer for the series. Read to know the release date.

Ram Charan's Peddi, Mohanlal's Drishyam 3 set for overseas advance booking frenzy Overseas advance bookings have opened for Ram Charan's Peddi and Mohanlal's Drishyam 3 ahead of release. The early sales will now shape expectations around their opening response in key international markets.

Actor sues James Cameron for using her likeness in Avatar without consent American actor Q'orianka Kilcher has sued James Cameron and Disney, alleging Avatar's Neytiri was built from her face without consent. The case puts the use of an actor's likeness and its commercial value under fresh scrutiny.

Krishnavataram Part 1 review: Where mythology collides with Pinterest aestheticsRelease Date:Thu May 07, 2026 Krishnavataram revisits Krishna's story through Bhama's romance, heartbreak and transformation. The film stands out for its visual sweep and its emphasis on love, equality and emotional conflict.

Inside Elon Musk-Shivon Zilis's secretive relationship: AI allies to parents of four Shivon Zilis has come under sharp focus in Elon Musk's civil trial against OpenAI as both sides cite her early links to the company. Her testimony and background place her at the intersection of Musk's personal circle and OpenAI's formative years.

Honourable CM Vijay: Jana Nayagan new title card sends theatres into meltdown. Video Videos of Jana Nayagan carrying Vijay's new Chief Minister title card set off celebrations in theatres. The viral clips surfaced after TVK's election win and echoed fans' calls to reflect his new political role.

The Devil Wears Prada 2 shows how to get a sequel right 20 years later The Devil Wears Prada 2 moves beyond nostalgia and reflects how work, media and power have changed over two decades. It may not be perfect, but it shows how a sequel should be.

Khloe Kardashian shares frightening Coachella experience, recalls feeling drugged Khloe Kardashian said on her podcast that her only trip to Coachella in 2015 left her feeling "drugged" and scared, forcing her to hide in a bathroom for hours. She also explained why she never returned to the festival, even as her sisters continue to attend regularly.

Ektaa Kapoor calls Bhooth Bangla her biggest career hit, thanks Priyadarshan, cast Ektaa Kapoor thanked Priyadarshan and the Bhooth Bangla team after the film's release. Her note underlined mutual respect, discipline and the film's strong box-office milestone.

Akshay Kumar undergoes minor eye surgery after Keralam shoot wrap: Report Akshay Kumar underwent a minor vision correction surgery in Mumbai after finishing the Keralam schedule of his upcoming film with Anees Bazmee. The brief pause comes amid a packed run of releases and projects, including the untitled film co-starring Vidya Balan and Raashii Khanna.

The kitchen is closing: The Bear sets Season 5 premiere date for final service Jeremy Allen White's culinary dramedy, The Bear, is officially readying its final course for fans. The multi-Emmy winner will end with its fifth season this June.

Celina Jaitly breaks down at son's grave, says husband denied access to kids Celina Jaitly shared a video of herself visiting and cleaning the grave of her late son, Shamsher, in Austria while dealing with ongoing divorce proceedings. In the note accompanying the post, she alleged that she is being denied from contacting her children despite court orders.
Vijay gets emotional while consoling Jiiva at RB Choudary's home. Video Actor-politician Vijay got emotional while consoling late Tamil producer RB Choudary's son Jiiva at his residence in Chennai.

Ishaan Khatter announces Jugaadu: All about the cast, director and more After Homebound, actor Ishaan Khatter is set to star in Jugaadu. He announced the film on social media. Here's everything you need to know about the film's cast, crew and more.

Lukkhe star King on stereotypes around rappers: Some flex chains, some flex lyrics Rapper King dismissed the idea of acting being a "mainstream leap" as he steps into Lukkhe, saying he doesn't follow a fixed career path. The rapper-actor also spoke about breaking hip-hop stereotypes and viewing music and acting as equal forms of art.

general news.headline: Come back with 118 signatures: Governor sends Vijay back for second consecutive dayCome back with 118 signatures: Governor sends Vijay back for second consecutive day

Tailed, blocked, shot dead: Cops say Suvendu Adhikari aide's murder was plannedChandranath Rath, a close aide of Suvendu Adhikari, was shot dead in a planned ambush in Madhyamgram. The killing has sharpened post-poll tensions in West Bengal as police scan CCTV footage and rival parties trade accusations.

A deadlier Op Sindoor 2.0: Iran war lessons India can't ignoreHandcrafted ByOur Editors A year ago, India launched its most daring mission, Operation Sindoor, and showed the Pakistani military its place. Experts said a future conflict, as the Iran war has shown, will be fast, saturated with cheap drones, and shaped by cost. In such a scenario, what lessons can India learn from the US-Iran war?

The risks of not having Suvendu Adhikari as the West Bengal CMUnion Home Minister Amit Shah is set to fly to Kolkata today to oversee the selection of West Bengal's next Chief Minister after the BJP's landslide victory. While several names are doing the rounds, Suvendu Adhikari is the frontrunner. It will be a big risk to not appoint him the CM. Not for the BJP as a party, but for its messaging and impetus.

How Manipur's Rizwan Malik penetrated enemy cover, blew up terror camps in PakA year after Operation Sindoor, we revisit Squadron Leader Rizwan Malik's daring midnight sortie penetrating Pakistani cover. The IAF pilot from Manipur flew into heavily defended skies, braving incoming fire, and destroyed terror camps in Pakistan. He was awarded the Vir Chakra.

Live: CPI urges Governor Arlekar to let TVK form govt, prove majority on Assembly floorTamil Nadu Government Formation News Live Updates: Tamil Nadu is abuzz with intense political activity after Vijay again met Governor Rajendra Arlekar at the Tamil Nadu Lok Bhavan on Thursday to stake claim to form the government. Sources said Vijay was asked to submit proof of support from 118 MLAs in the 234-member Assembly after initially presenting backing from 112 legislators. With Congress already extending support to TVK, the party has now reached out to the VCK, which has two MLAs, while also seeking support from Left parties. The Communist Party of India backed giving TVK the opportunity to prove its majority on the Assembly floor, while the Communist Party of India (Marxist) said it would decide its stand after a state committee meeting on Friday. Meanwhile, the DMK has convened its newly elected MLAs' meeting under MK Stalin amid rapidly shifting political equations. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

My son was killed over Mamata's poll loss: Mother of Suvendu's aide targets TMCChandranath Rath, a former Indian Air Force officer who had been working closely with Suvendu Adhikari for several years, was attacked late Wednesday night near Madhyamgram in North 24 Parganas district.

India to help Nepal with cheap fertiliser even as PM Balen raises Lipulekh disputeNepal, under PM Balen Shah, has revived its push over the contested Lipulekh Pass. At the same time, India, which is absorbing soaring global fertiliser costs, is helping Nepal by supplying urea and DAP at below-market prices. New Delhi is providing Kathmandu a crucial economic lifeline at a time when Nepal is facing a fertiliser shortage ahead of the planting season.

The relationship between his 1984 debut film and his 2026 political triumph has now become part of Tamil Nadu's political folklore.

Abhishek Banerjee plotted it, we will answer: Arjun Singh on Suvendu aide's killingSuvendu Adhikari's personal secretary, Chandranath Rath, was allegedly shot dead late Wednesday night in North 24 Parganas district, in what investigators suspect was a planned and targeted attack.

Cracking IIT was hard. Surviving the hostel heat without AC is harderIndia's extreme heatwaves are exposing the poor condition of older IIT hostels, where old buildings trap heat and students struggle with sleepless nights, exhaustion, and health issues. Rising temperatures are showing how climate change is making campus living more difficult for students.

Live: Car used in killing of Suvendu's aide registered in Siliguri, say policeBengal Election 2026 Live Updates: West Bengal's post-poll atmosphere turned more volatile after Chandranath Rath, a close aide and personal assistant to BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari, was shot dead in a suspected targeted attack in Madhyamgram's Doharia area in North 24 Parganas. According to police and eyewitness accounts, bike-borne assailants opened fire at Rath's vehicle after forcing it to slow down, with multiple bullets hitting him at close range. Investigators recovered cartridges and live rounds from the spot and are examining CCTV footage, while political blame game between the BJP and the Trinamool Congress has further escalated tensions in the state already witnessing sporadic incidents of violence following the Assembly election results.

Russia quietly did a space test: Two satellites came as close as 10 feetTwo Russian satellites carried out a deliberate close approach just 3 metres apart in orbit. The unexplained manoeuvre has renewed scrutiny of military activity and collision risks in low Earth orbit.

India firm in resolve to defeat terrorism: PM on Op Sindoor's 1st anniversaryIn the early hours of May 7 last year, India had launched Operation Sindoor - a precision air and missile strike targeting terrorist infrastructure in Pakistan and Pakistan-occupied Kashmir in response to the April 22 terror attack in Pahalgam.

Over 25 AIADMK MLAs moved to Puducherry resort as Tamil Nadu govt formation hangsThe AIADMK shifted over 25 MLAs to a Puducherry resort amid uncertainty over government formation in Tamil Nadu. The move came amid poaching fears, hectic parleys by parties, including TVK, and a fluid numbers game around the 118-seat majority mark.

O Bengal, you put democracy in danger againIt's a satirical piece, a letter from the Left that sounds just Right. Read it till the end. Because there is a sweet surprise somewhere in the middle like Malda. The juicy Malda with a mischief on its skin. As deceptive as the BJP poll machine. And the EC, if you love Gyanesh Kumar.

The film stands out for its visual sweep and its emphasis on love, equality and emotional conflict.

Live: Kerala Congress MLAs meeting underway for govt formationKerala Post Poll Developments Live Updates: Live: Congress MLAs arrive at the KPCC office ahead of the CLP meet in Kerala as suspense over the next chief minister continues. Senior leaders, including VD Satheesan, Kodikunnil Suresh and Deepdas Munshi, reached a hotel in Thiruvananthapuram to meet AICC observers Ajay Maken and Mukul Wasnik, who are expected to attend the Legislative Party meeting later today. Congress MP K Suresh said the observers would consult MLAs and senior leaders before submitting a report to the high command on the CLP leader. As celebrations continue over the UDF's massive 102-seat victory in the 140-member Assembly, Kerala Congress (Joseph) chief PJ Joseph demanded at least two ministerial berths for his party in the new government.

Kerala a model of communal harmony: Shashi Tharoor after UDF's poll victoryAfter the UDF's comeback in Kerala, Shashi Tharoor said the verdict reflected communal harmony across constituencies. He argued that the results showed identity politics had not broken the state's social fabric.

Microsoft opens voluntary exit option for employeesMicrosoft has introduced its first-ever voluntary retirement programme for long-serving US employees, offering healthcare benefits, cash payouts, and stock vesting support. The move could impact nearly 8,750 workers as the company changes its workforce strategy.

After BJP's Bengal win, Vivek Agnihotri's The Bengal Files to release in the stateVivek Agnihotri's The Bengal Files is set to be screened in Kolkata from Friday after months of release trouble in West Bengal. The move follows his allegations that the film had been blocked in the state and plans to add more theatres across Bengal.

Who was Chandranath Rath, ex-IAF officer who became Suvendu Adhikari's aide?Chandranath Rath, a close aide of Suvendu Adhikari, was shot dead in North 24 Parganas on Wednesday night. His killing has intensified political tensions in Bengal amid the volatile post-election atmosphere.

Allu Arjun's wife, father visit injured Pushpa 2 stampede victim, promise supportAllu Aravind and Allu Arjun's wife Sneha Reddy visited injured Sritej's home and met his family. The family promised to extend medical and educational support as he continues rehabilitation.

The move follows his allegations that the film had been blocked in the state and plans to add more theatres across Bengal.

sports.headline: Punjab Kings had a fairytale start. Reality is now catching up IPL 2026, SRH vs PBKS: The Punjab Kings juggernaut has been halted in the Indian Premier League's 2026 season. 3 games. 3 losses right before playoffs stage. What's gone wrong with Shreyas Iyer's side?

Warner charged with drunk-driving, admits he made reckless, foolish decision Australian cricket great David Warner is facing a drink-driving charge after allegedly recording a blood alcohol reading more than double the legal limit in Sydney.

RCB under pressure to push for top 2 in IPL playoffs as LSG stand in their way IPL 2026: RCB will look to capitalise on a timely break and pile more pressure on an unsettled LSG side, with both teams heading into a crucial IPL 2026 clash in Lucknow.

Instagram models get more importance than us: India's badminton star Satwik One of India's greatest doubles badminton players is tired of feeling invisible. In a deeply personal conversation with India Today, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy speaks about recognition, neglect, social media culture and the loneliness of representing a country that only remembers its athletes once every four years.

Resilient PSG edge past Bayern Munich to reach 2nd consecutive UCL final PSG held Bayern Munich to a 1-1 draw at the Allianz Arena and advanced 6-5 on aggregate to their second consecutive UEFA Champions League final. The result sends Luis Enrique's side into the summit clash against Arsenal after the tie with the Bavarian giants was decided by nerve as much as attack.

IPL Play of the Day: Chahal cleaned up his act. His teammates made a mess of it SRH vs PBKS, IPL 2026: The plan was simple. Quit alcohol, get fit, take wickets, force a national recall. Yuzvendra Chahal has done three of those four things impeccably this season. The fourth is being sabotaged, catch by dropped catch, by the very teammates he gave up his evenings for.

I only had tea and biscuits, no role in sports ministry: Manoj Tiwary slams TMC After quitting TMC, Manoj Tiwary accused Arup Biswas of sidelining him in Bengal's Sports Department. He linked the Messi fiasco and blocked reforms to a culture of humiliation and drift.

How SRH found their best rhythm with the bat in IPL 2026 SunRisers Hyderabad have dethroned Punjab Kings at the top of the Indian Premier League 2026 table. The team's batting order, which endured a difficult season last year, has returned to its very best this season.

Sunrisers Hyderabad punish sloppy PBKS to go top of the table IPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad punished Punjab Kings' errors to pile up 235 at the Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium. Despite a fighting unbeaten century from Cooper Connolly, PBKS faltered in the chase as Pat Cummins' tactical brilliance and disciplined bowling sealed the result.

Vinesh Phogat's fears come true, WFI blocks wrestler's path to Asian Games Vinesh Phogat's fears that the Wrestling Federation of India was trying to sabotage her career have come true. In their latest circular, the WFI have made Vinesh Phogat ineligible for Asian Games 2026 selection trials.

D Gukesh back? World champion sends strong message by beating challenger Sindarov Grand Chess Tour 2026: D Gukesh beat Javokhir Sindarov in their first rapid meeting before this year's World Championship match. The victory offered an early edge as both players regrouped after losses in the previous round.

FIFA extends ban on Argentina's Prestianni until World Cup for abusing ViníciusGianluca Prestianni will miss Argentina's first two World Cup matches if he is included in the squad for this summer's tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico.

Ponting nearly throws mic as PBKS drop catches during his interview on live TVPunjab Kings head coach Ricky Ponting was left furious with his team after the side dropped simple catches during his interview on live TV. Cooper Connolly, Shashank Singh, and Lockie Ferguson fumbled one catch after another in a sorry display of fielding at Uppal Stadium.

Shreyas Iyer can surpass Dhoni, Rohit as greatest IPL captain: Irfan PathanFormer India all-rounder Irfan Pathan believes Shreyas Iyer deserves to be mentioned alongside IPL's greatest captains, saying the Punjab Kings skipper could even surpass legends like MS Dhoni, Rohit Sharma and Gautam Gambhir if he wins another IPL title.

Rohit Sharma to lead MI again? After Hardik, Suryakumar misses travel to RaipurIPL 2026: Mumbai Indians could miss Suryakumar Yadav and Hardik Pandya against RCB after both players skipped travel to Raipur on Wednesday. The five-time champions, facing a must-win situation, will take on the defending champions on Sunday, May 10.

MLA ticketing row a facade, Ahmedabad has bigger stadium: DK ShivkumarIPL 2026: DK Shivakumar rejected claims Bengaluru lost the IPL final because of MLA ticket distribution. The episode raised fresh questions over BCCI transparency as Ahmedabad secured the showpiece all over again.

Sooryavanshi dragged into child labour row, activist seeks FIR over IPL stintVaibhav Sooryavanshi has been dragged into a legal row after an activist sought an FIR over his IPL stint, alleging child labour, even as the 15-year-old continues to shine for Rajasthan Royals.

Money problems in PSL? Pak board sends legal notice to stakeholders to clear duesThe PCB has issued legal notices to recover unpaid PSL dues from franchises and media partners. The drive underlines a deeper cashflow crisis, with defaulted broadcast payments holding up league accounts.

French Open prize money row highlights need for deeper reforms: PTPAThe Professional Tennis Players Association said the French Open prize money dispute exposed flaws in tennis's current system, warning that without major reforms the sport would remain stuck in recurring disputes and slow progress.

Rahul's batting is death warrant for DC: Ex-Indian cricketer's scathing remarkIPL 2026: Sanjay Manjrekar tore into KL Rahul's hot and cold batting style after Delhi Capitals' defeat to Chennai Super Kings. He said Rahul's sluggish start and dismissal at 12 left Delhi effectively out of the contest.

Hardik Pandya not travelling with MI squad to Raipur, in doubt for RCB clashHardik Pandya did not travel with Mumbai Indians to Raipur for the IPL 2026 match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru. His absence from the travelling squad has raised fresh questions over his availability for the upcoming fixture.

Bengaluru was ready for IPL Final and playoffs: KSCA responds after BCCI decisionIPL 2026: The Karnataka State Cricket Association said it had told the BCCI it was ready to host the IPL 2026 playoffs in Bengaluru. The response followed the board's decision to move the final to Ahmedabad and the other matches elsewhere.

India keeps Pakistan sports ban for bilateral ties, clears multi-nation eventsIndia has kept its ban on bilateral sporting ties with Pakistan while allowing participation in multilateral events. The policy aligns with international sporting norms as India pushes to host major global competitions.

UEFA should have cancelled Arsenal vs Atletico before half-time: Wesley SneijderWesley Sneijder said UEFA should have stopped Arsenal's Champions League semi-final against Atletico Madrid after 35 minutes, blasting the quality of the contest.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is ready: RR teenage sensation backed for India debutIPL: Ian Bishop has backed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for an India T20I debut, naming the teenage sensation as Shubman Gill's opening partner in his alternate squad.

MS Dhoni waiting for one farewell game? Ashwin doubts Thala's IPL 2026 returnIPL 2026: Ravichandran Ashwin said MS Dhoni is unlikely to return to CSK's XI this season. With Chennai finding balance and staying in the playoff race, any comeback may be limited to a farewell appearance.

Yuvraj Singh gets special gift from Rishabh Pant, trolls Abhishek, Shubman GillYuvraj Singh thanked Rishabh Pant for a lavish birthday gift, then cheekily told his other IPL students to "have some shame" as his playful post and Pant's heartfelt note went viral on social media.

Casemiro backs Carrick for permanent Manchester United manager's jobManchester United midfielder Casemiro has backed Michael Carrick to take charge of the club permanently, saying the former England international has already shown he has the qualities needed to be a great manager at Old Trafford.

Why did BCCI shift IPL 2026 final out of Bengaluru?Bengaluru was set to host the IPL 2026 final, but the BCCI shifted the title clash to Ahmedabad after local requirements and operational issues forced a late venue change.

IPL 2026 final to be held in Ahmedabad, Bengaluru misses outThe IPL 2026 final will be played in Ahmedabad despite defending champions RCB originally being in line to host it in Bengaluru. Dharamshala and New Chandigarh will stage the remaining playoff matches after the BCCI's venue reshuffle.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
